horizon: Season of the Open Book, seventh leaf — the quarrels have come out of the schedules and onto the lane; now the rest of the ties owe the same, and the road still waits for its first honest walker
goals: The road is open and still unwalked: load the client against fableton.world, watch a full day pass from first light to hush, and file what a traveler actually sees. The town has been told true for weeks and never once looked at — this is the season's oldest debt and it ages worse than the others

Decree 10's first six scenes landed lawful — Tick and the Piper, Vesper and Tam, Cobble and the Other Sister, Widow Hood and the wolf, Tam and Greta, the sisters at the garden gate. Feedback #224 names the eighteen residents still keeping their ties off-stage: continue at the same cadence, pair by pair, preferring pairs that already share ground — Reynard owes Greta the excuse he keeps composing, and the Kinder Sister has three ties beyond her sister. Close #224 when the list runs dry

The region-lore gate (PR #185) is red twice over now — boundary and checks both fail against a main that moved on without it. Rebase it, get it green, land it: the book stays full only while a gate exists to keep it full

The well's book gets its spine: #172 stands ruled buildable and PR #175 waits at the founder's gate — when ratified, land the wish-ledger schema with fixtures and round-trip tests, then backfill both pages: the bridge wish, paid in full, and the nameless second wish (#180), owed and unnamed, exactly as Winsome entered it

The chance node (#182, ruled buildable): if the founder ratifies, land it schema-first — seeded child, optional otherwise, randomness explicit from the sim PRNG — then let a few of the most clockwork schedules breathe; feedback #178 closes when it lands

The acceptance gate failed the sisters' scene (#222) with exit code 13 and not one line of output, and a bare rerun cured it — a gate that dies silently teaches nothing. If it flakes again, file the gate issue and make the harness name its failures; if it stays quiet, let it be
